text: Strategic roadmap schedules Fab 2 for 2nm nanosheet production in 2028 and Fab 3 for 2nm or more advanced nodes by 2030; CEO Wei confirms advanced tech deployment will prioritize Taiwan over US sites.**

CONFLICT: Source 0 and 2 report Arizona 4nm yields "on par" or "similar" to Taiwan, while Source 2 explicitly cites Rick Cassidy (President, TSMC US) stating yields are 4% better than Taiwan facilities.**
Logistics costs for US operations are significantly higher than Taiwan; critical chemicals like sulfuric acid are currently shipped from Taiwan to Los Angeles then trucked to Arizona.**

TSMC Arizona total investment reached $65B for a three-fab campus, supported by $6.6B in US CHIPS Act grants and $5B in loans; project requires 6,000 permanent employees and 20,000 construction workers.**

Operational friction attributed to permitting timelines at least 2x longer than Taiwan, lack of established US chip-plant regulations, and "cultural clashes" between relocated Taiwanese engineers and US staff.**
CONFLICT: Sources 0 and 2 cite volume production delayed from 2024 to "early 2025" due to labor shortages; Source 1 (dated Jan 21, 2025) reports 4nm mass production has already commenced.**
